filter_pushdown.note: lineitem sorted by l_shipdate; zone map skips ~8 of 600 tail blocks (block_min > 10471), saving ~1.3% of I/O

group_by.note: At most 6 groups — use fixed 6-slot flat array, no hash table needed

per_group: count        (uint64) — for COUNT(*) and AVG denominators

output_sort.note: Only 6 rows — no TopK heap, just sort the merged accumulator array

data_structures.thread_local_accumulators: each thread owns one flat_array(6_slots) of struct{double sum_qty, sum_base, sum_disc_price, sum_charge, sum_disc; uint64_t count}
data_structures.merge: sequential reduction of 64 thread-local arrays into one final 6-slot array — O(64*6) trivial

parallelism.note: No contention during scan/accumulate phase; single-threaded merge of 64x6 accumulators at end is negligible

index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=600][int32_t min, int32_t max per block]

vectorization.fused_ops: load l_shipdate → compare → mask → load 5 payload columns → multiply/accumulate into group slot
vectorization.note: Fuse filter check and all 5 accumulator updates in a single vectorized inner loop per morsel

io_strategy.rationale: Selectivity is 98.7% (>> 50% threshold) — must read nearly all blocks; zone_map_guided_selective not warranted except for tail skip
io_strategy.prefetch_note: Issue concurrent madvise(MADV_WILLNEED) for all 7 column files using OpenMP to saturate HDD I/O bandwidth; zone map is tiny (4.8 KB) and loaded first synchronously to determine qualifying byte ranges for each column

sampling.reason: Q1 has zero joins — sampling is only mandatory for queries with 2+ joins

estimated_cost.data_volume_bytes: ~3.36 GB (7 cols x 8 bytes x 60M rows)
estimated_cost.notes: Hot run is CPU/memory-bandwidth bound (~105ms target matching observed GenDB hot). Cold run is HDD-I/O bound (~2060ms). Parallel prefetch + zone-map tail-skip + single-pass fused AVX512 scan are the key levers.
